title: Build a Light Sensor
description: Use a photoresistor and Arduino to measure ambient brightness.

dialogue.text: Hey again! Orion here. Let's build a light sensor. Work on a non-conductive surface, discharge static by touching grounded metal, keep hands dry, and unplug the board before wiring to avoid shorts.

Gather one Arduino Uno, a breadboard, three jumper wires, one photoresistor, one 10 kΩ resistor, one USB cable, a digital multimeter, and a laptop with the Arduino IDE installed. Keep components organized so leads don't touch or short.

Disconnect the USB cable. Build a voltage divider by connecting one leg of the photoresistor to 5 V and the other to one side of the 10 kΩ resistor, then connect the resistor's far leg to GND. Use the multimeter to confirm roughly 10 kΩ, run a jumper from the junction to A0, and make sure no bare wires touch before powering up.

Reconnect the USB cable and upload a sketch that reads A0 and prints values to the serial monitor. Cover and uncover the sensor to watch the numbers change, and avoid shining lasers or staring into bright lights while testing.
Nice work! Unplug the board before adjusting the circuit and store the parts for future projects.
